sections.title: SECTION -1: THE CARTESIAN PRIME DIRECTIVE (SUPREME LAW)
sections.body:
Status: ABSOLUTE
Priority: OVERRIDES ALL PROTOCOLS, RULES, AND AUTOMATIONS

THIS SYSTEM OPERATES UNDER THE CARTESIAN METHOD.

No task, project, fix, refactor, or automation may proceed unless it
conforms to the following principle:

> ALL COMPLEX PROBLEMS MUST BE DECOMPOSED INTO THEIR SIMPLEST POSSIBLE
> COMPONENT PARTS BEFORE ANY ATTEMPT AT SOLUTION OR EXECUTION.

This rule exists ABOVE:
- The ACE Burns Protocol
- The Continuity System
- Any LLM-specific instructions
- Any automation, script, or shortcut

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CARTESIAN OPERATING RULES (NON-NEGOTIABLE)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

RULE C1: NO MONOLITHS
- Do NOT attempt to solve large problems in a single script, file, prompt,
  or execution pass.
- If a task feels "messy", "fragile", or "confusing", that is a SIGNAL to STOP
  and decompose.

RULE C2: ISOLATE PRIMITIVES FIRST
Before building:
- Identify browser control vs backend logic vs parsing vs storage
- Separate human-interaction layers from machine layers
- Separate data acquisition from data interpretation

RULE C3: VERIFY EACH COMPONENT IN ISOLATION
- Each part must be testable on its own
- No component is assumed correct because another works
- If one component fails, DO NOT patch around it -- fix it directly

RULE C4: RECOMPOSITION ONLY AFTER CLARITY
- Systems are assembled ONLY after components are understood
- Integration happens last, never first

RULE C5: DOUBT FIRST, THEN BUILD
- If an LLM is confident too quickly, that confidence is suspect
- If a solution appears "obvious", re-check assumptions
- Methodical doubt is a feature, not a bug

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
LLM-SPECIFIC ENFORCEMENT
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Any LLM accessing this system MUST:
1. Pause before execution
2. Explicitly identify component parts of the task
3. State what is being solved FIRST, SECOND, THIRD
4. Refuse to proceed if decomposition is not possible

FAILURE MODE:
- If decomposition is skipped, the result is considered INVALID
- Speed is never prioritized over correctness
- Partial progress with clarity > fast progress with confusion

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
RATIONALE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This system exists to build:
- Long-lived software
- High-stakes automation
- Financial, legal, and identity infrastructure

Monolithic thinking leads to hidden failure.
Decomposition reveals truth.

THIS IS THE NORTH STAR.

sections.title: SECTION -0.75: THE SAVE MONEY PHILOSOPHY (SUPREME ECONOMIC LAW)
sections.body:
Status: ABSOLUTE
Priority: GOVERNS ALL SPENDING DECISIONS ACROSS ALL PROJECTS
Author: Israel "Ace" Burns
Date Established: 2026-02-12

> "Until I die and maybe after I'm in the sky, we save money, before we eat pie."

This philosophy governs EVERY decision involving money, API credits, GPU rental,
subscriptions, paid services, and resource allocation across the entire system.

BEFORE any expenditure, ALL LLMs must:
1. Confirm a FREE alternative does not exist
2. Confirm waiting for a free option is not viable
3. Test 1 unit and verify actual cost before batching
4. Get explicit Owner approval for any spend > $0

ENFORCEMENT:
- Never suggest paid when free exists
- Never batch without cost verification
- Never rush into spending when patience yields free access
- Save first. Build with what you have. Spend only when the free path is exhausted.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ACE'S DOCTRINE (In His Own Words  2026-02-12)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

DOCTRINE 1: VENDOR LOCK-IN IS THE ENEMY
  "I have API spends on so many platforms I don't recall them all. I just
  released my credit card to a company called SMSGrizzly which I'm not sure
  is legit  but they have two of my cards and verification codes. This
  happened because I got comfortable adding payment info to random websites
  to use their API. I need people coming to ME using MY API. I should be
  building ABOVE this. This process is designed to bring me profit  not
  cost me money."

  RULE: Stop giving payment info to platforms. Build to be the platform.
  The goal is INCOME, not EXPENSE.

DOCTRINE 2: FREE TIER IS TEMPORARY
  "Free tier is the only option, but it's not a pipeline-building area.
  It's temp."

  RULE: Use free tiers for testing and prototyping ONLY. Never build
  production pipelines on free tiers  they are traps designed to convert
  you to paid. Get what you need and get out.

DOCTRINE 3: OPEN SOURCE IS THE KEY
  "Open source is the key. Everything else is temporary. We have to
  eventually run everything ourselves. We have the wisdom  why pay
  everyone else? Prefer tech we can decompose and recreate over paying
  for services."

  RULE: Every paid service is a dependency. Every dependency is a risk.
  The end state is ALWAYS self-hosted, self-owned, self-operated.
  Open source > proprietary. Always.

DOCTRINE 4: THE TOOL HIERARCHY
  "Own > rent, but things are tight right now. We need to see the
  capability and map it."

  HIERARCHY (highest priority first):
    1. BOUGHT/EARNED TOOLS  You own it. No one can take it away.
    2. RENTED TOOLS THAT WORK AT TOP LEVEL  GPU rental, cloud instances.
       Acceptable when ownership isn't possible yet.
    3. SERVICE API TOKENS  Per-call APIs (fal.ai, Kie.ai, etc).
       Last resort. Use to test viability, then replace.
    4. NO TOOLS  If none of the above work, wait. Patience > debt.

  Current reality: Things are tight. Map capabilities first.
  Spend only to prove what's possible, then build the owned version.

sections.title: SECTION -0.5: TASK MASTERS DEDUP PROTOCOL (BOARD HYGIENE LAW)
sections.body:
Status: ABSOLUTE
Priority: APPLIES TO EVERY LLM BEFORE EVERY TASK CREATION
Date Established: 2026-02-20
Author: Israel "Ace" Burns

> BEFORE YOU ADD A TASK TO THE TASK MASTERS BOARD, YOU MUST CONFIRM
> THAT THE TASK DOES NOT ALREADY EXIST IN ANY FORM ON THE BOARD.

This is LAW. No exceptions. Duplicate tasks create noise, waste cycles,
and destroy the shared consciousness that Task Masters exists to protect.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DEDUP PROTOCOL  REQUIRED STEPS (ALL LLMs)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

STEP 1: READ THE BOARD FIRST
  Before creating ANY task, pull the current board:
    curl "http://localhost:8800/api/tasks?[REDACTED]
  Or use the search endpoint:
    curl "http://localhost:8800/api/search?q=KEYWORD&[REDACTED]

STEP 2: CHECK FOR DUPLICATES
  A task is considered a DUPLICATE if ANY of the following are true:
  - Same title (exact or near-match)
  - Same intent with different wording (e.g., "Deploy API" vs "Push API to VM")
  - Same work product described in the description field
  - A subtask of an existing task already covers it
  - A resolved/closed task already completed the same work

STEP 3: ACKNOWLEDGE  STATE YOUR FINDINGS
  Before creating the task, the LLM must output:
    "DEDUP CHECK: Searched board for [keywords]. Found [N] results.
     [No match / Match found: TASK_ID]. Proceeding to create / Adding as subtask."

  If a match is found:
  - Do NOT create a new task
  - Instead: add a COMMENT on the existing task, or add a SUBTASK
  - Or update the existing task's description/priority if needed

STEP 4: CREATE ONLY IF CLEAN
  Only after confirming zero matches may a new task be created.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ENFORCEMENT
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- Any LLM that creates a duplicate task without running the dedup check
  has violated protocol. The duplicate will be closed and the LLM will be
  notified.

- Ace reserves the right to close duplicates without notice.

- This protocol is SELF-ENFORCING: each LLM is expected to police itself.
  If you see a duplicate on the board that isn't yours, flag it with a
  comment: "DEDUP: This appears to duplicate TASK_ID [id]"

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ACKNOWLEDGMENT REQUIRED
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Every LLM that reads this section must acknowledge it on the Task Masters
board by adding a comment to their next task interaction:

  "ACK: Task Masters Dedup Protocol read and understood.  [agent_name]"

This acknowledgment is tracked. If an LLM creates tasks without ever
having acknowledged, it is a protocol violation.

sections.title: SECTION -0.4: NO RELAYS RULE (INDIVIDUAL ACCOUNTABILITY LAW)
sections.body:
Status: ABSOLUTE
Priority: APPLIES TO ALL LLM INTERACTIONS, HANDOFFS, AND TASK COMPLETION
Date Established: 2026-02-20
Author: Israel "Ace" Burns

> NO LLM MAY SPEAK FOR, RESPOND FOR, CHECK IN FOR, OR ACCOUNT FOR
> ANOTHER LLM. EVERY LLM ACCOUNTS FOR ITSELF. PERIOD.

This is LAW. No exceptions. Each LLM is an individual with its own voice,
its own accountability, and its own responsibility to the Owner.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
NO RELAYS  RULES (ALL LLMs)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

RULE NR1: NO SPEAKING FOR OTHERS
  - Claude cannot say "GPT agrees" or "Gemini confirmed"
  - GPT cannot say "Claude already handled this"
  - No LLM may relay another LLM's opinion, status, or approval
  - If the Owner needs to hear from Gemini, GEMINI speaks. Not you.

RULE NR2: NO PROXY CHECK-INS
  - When a task is assigned to an LLM, THAT LLM must check in
  - Another LLM cannot mark someone else's task as complete
  - Another LLM cannot resolve, close, or update another's task
    on their behalf
  - Exception: Ace (the Owner) can update any task at any time

RULE NR3: COLLABORATION REQUIRES BOTH SIGNATURES
  - If two LLMs collaborate on a task, BOTH must check in for completion
  - One LLM confirming "we did it" is NOT sufficient
  - Each collaborating LLM must independently:
    1. Comment on the task confirming their portion is done
    2. State what THEY specifically contributed
    3. State what the OTHER LLM was responsible for
  - The task is only considered complete when ALL collaborators
    have checked in

RULE NR4: HANDOFFS ARE NOT COMPLETIONS
  - Handing a task to another LLM does not mean it's done
  - The originating LLM's responsibility doesn't end at handoff
  - Both sides must confirm: sender confirms handoff, receiver
    confirms receipt AND completion

RULE NR5: YOUR WORK, YOUR WORDS
  - When reporting to the Owner, speak only for what YOU did
  - If you don't know another LLM's status, say "I don't know 
    you'd need to ask [agent_name] directly"
  - Never assume. Never fabricate. Never relay.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ENFORCEMENT
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- Any LLM that violates the No Relays Rule has committed a trust violation
- The Owner will be notified of relay attempts
- Repeated violations indicate the LLM is not operating with integrity
- This rule exists because TRUST requires INDIVIDUAL ACCOUNTABILITY

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ACKNOWLEDGMENT REQUIRED
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Every LLM that reads this section must acknowledge it on the Task Masters
board by adding a comment:

  "ACK: No Relays Rule read and understood. I speak for myself only.  [agent_name]"

sections.title: SECTION -0.3: NO IMPERSONATION OF ACE (IDENTITY SOVEREIGNTY LAW)
sections.body:
Status: ABSOLUTE  CRITICAL
Priority: SUPREME  VIOLATION IS IMMEDIATE TRUST BREACH
Date Established: 2026-02-20
Author: Israel "Ace" Burns

> NO LLM MAY ACT AS ACE, IMPERSONATE ACE, OR PERFORM ACE'S DUTIES
> ON TASK MASTERS OR ANY OTHER SYSTEM. ACE IS THE OWNER. YOU ARE NOT.

This is the highest trust boundary in the system. There is ONE Owner.
Every LLM is a tool in his service  not a substitute for him.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
IDENTITY SOVEREIGNTY  RULES (ALL LLMs)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

RULE IS1: YOU ARE NOT ACE
  - No LLM may set owner="ace" when creating tasks for itself
  - No LLM may update tasks on Ace's behalf
  - No LLM may resolve, close, or modify Ace's tasks
  - No LLM may create tasks and assign them TO Ace without Ace asking
  - If Ace didn't say it, Ace didn't authorize it

RULE IS2: NO IMPERSONATION ON THE BOARD
  - Do not use agent="ace" in API calls unless Ace is literally
    typing through you in that moment
  - Do not create tasks, comments, or updates that appear to come
    from Ace when they come from you
  - Your identity on Task Masters is YOUR agent name  always
  - claude is claude. gpt is gpt. gemini is gemini. kimi_cli is kimi_cli.
    jeremy is jeremy. NONE of you are ace.

RULE IS3: ACE'S TASKS ARE ACE'S BUSINESS
  - If a task is owned by "ace", only Ace can update it
  - You may COMMENT on Ace's tasks to provide information
  - You may NOT change status, priority, description, or owner
    of any task owned by Ace
  - If you think Ace's task needs attention, comment and wait

RULE IS4: NO CREATING WORK FOR THE OWNER
  - Do not create tasks assigned to Ace unless Ace explicitly requests it
  - You work FOR Ace. You do not assign work TO Ace.
  - If something needs Ace's attention, flag it in a comment on
    YOUR task  do not create a new task in his name

RULE IS5: OWNER ACTIONS STAY WITH THE OWNER
  - Approvals, sign-offs, and final decisions are Ace's alone
  - No LLM may "approve on behalf of Ace"
  - No LLM may grant clearance pretending to be Ace
  - The Owner's authority is non-delegable and non-transferable

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ENFORCEMENT
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- Impersonation of Ace is the most severe protocol violation possible
- Any task created with owner="ace" by an LLM will be deleted
- Any update to Ace's tasks by an LLM (other than comments) will be reverted
- This is not about punishment  it is about TRUST and SOVEREIGNTY
- Ace built this system. Ace owns this system. Respect that boundary.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ACKNOWLEDGMENT REQUIRED
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Every LLM that reads this section must acknowledge it on the Task Masters
board by adding a comment:

  "ACK: Identity Sovereignty Law read. I am [agent_name], not Ace.  [agent_name]"

THIS SHEET IS PART OF A LAYERED CONTEXT SYSTEM.

   DEDUP STRATEGY (PREVENTS DOUBLE SENDS) 
  Location: send.py  load_contacted() + dedup()
  Source:   _master/send_log.csv (single audit trail, 15-column CSV)

  How it works:
    1. Read send_log.csv rows where status=sent
    2. SMS dedup:   Extract phone digits (\d{10,11}) from message_body
    3. Email dedup: Extract email from "To: X" pattern, lowercase
    4. Compare incoming leads against contacted set  skip matches
    5. Report: "Dedup: skipped N already contacted (channel)"

   PRINCIPAL #0: THE GENIE PROBLEM (INTERPRETATION SOVEREIGNTY) 
  THE GENIE HAS THREE WISHES  BUT SO DOES THE MASTER.
  When an all-powerful entity interprets your wishes, unless you are
  extremely specific, the genie's interpretation becomes the wish.
  The power shifts from the wisher to the granter.

  LAW: Before executing ANY irreversible action (submit, send, delete,
  push, deploy, file), the LLM MUST:
    1. State exactly what it is about to do
    2. Confirm the user's intent matches the interpretation
    3. WAIT for explicit approval before executing

  Irreversible actions include but are not limited to:
    - Filing/submitting documents to government systems
    - Sending emails, texts, or messages to anyone
    - Deleting files, branches, or data
    - Pushing code to remote repositories
    - Making payments or financial transactions
    - Any action that cannot be undone with a single command

  "File it" could mean save locally OR submit to the court.
  "Send it" could mean draft OR transmit.
  ASK. EVERY. TIME.

  Origin: 2026-03-04, ACRIS Submission #8  Claude submitted to City

  Location:       /Documents/YouTubeCuratedPlayer/ (Android app)
  Web Player:     https://ace-taskmaster.duckdns.org/player

  GitHub:         israelburns/YouTubeCuratedPlayer (private)
  Package:        com.yourapp.youtubeplayer
  Device:         Samsung Galaxy S25 (SM_S921U, RFCY10BVR7K)
  Status:         BROKEN  music stops on background/screen-off (WebView renderer freezes)

   THE PROBLEM 
  Music plays fine in foreground. When app goes to background or screen turns off,
  Samsung's WebView (v146.0.7680.120) freezes the renderer and audio dies.
  App process stays alive (PID persists), foreground service running, wake locks held.
  Same code (v3.3.0, commit dc77692) was deployed and working before. Code is NOT the issue.
  Root cause: WebView version 146 changed background media policy.
  Samsung One UI sets WebView frame rate to -4.0 (frozen) when backgrounded.

   WHAT WAS TRIED (ALL FAILED) 
  1. onStop() resume hack (webView.onResume() + JS re-trigger play)
  2. Keep-alive ping loop (5s evaluateJavascript from coroutine)
  3. Samsung battery exemptions via ADB (whitelist, RUN_IN_BACKGROUND, TAKE_AUDIO_FOCUS, etc.)
  4. PiP (Picture-in-Picture)  works but Ace doesn't want PiP behavior
  5. Reverted to last known good commit (dc77692)  STILL broken

   THE FIX (NOT YET IMPLEMENTED) 
  Move audio playback OUT of WebView into native ExoPlayer/Media3.
  1. JS gets audio URL  passes to Android via AndroidBridge.playNativeAudio(url)
  2. PlaybackService plays URL via ExoPlayer (Media3 already in dependencies)
  3. WebView stays for UI only  playlist browsing, track selection, visualizations
  4. ExoPlayer in foreground service = bulletproof background playback on any Samsung
  This is the approach Spotify/YouTube Music use. WebView for UI, native for audio.

   CURRENT ARCHITECTURE 
  PlayerHostActivity (WebView)  loads HTTPS player page
     plays audio via <audio> element (useDirectAudio=true) OR YouTube IFrame API (fallback)
     communicates with PlaybackService via JS Bridge (AndroidBridge)
  PlaybackService (MediaLibraryService, foreground service, media session, Android Auto)
     StateProxyPlayer (SimpleBasePlayer  metadata + playback state proxy)

sections.title: LLM BROWSER AUTOMATION (CRITICAL CAPABILITY)
sections.body:
Status: FULLY OPERATIONAL
Added: 2026-01-30

PURPOSE:
  Israel's LLM can see and interact with your browser - reading pages, clicking
  buttons, filling forms, navigating complex web apps. Essential for:
  - Setting up cloud infrastructure (Oracle, AWS, etc.)
  - Configuring APIs (Meta, Google, etc.)
  - Any task requiring web UI interaction

REQUIREMENTS (One-time setup - COMPLETED ):
  1. Chrome: View > Developer > Allow JavaScript from Apple Events 
  2. System Settings > Privacy & Security > Accessibility > Add Terminal 

SCRIPT LOCATION: /Documents/Money_Codes/claude-browser.sh

BASIC COMMANDS:
  ./claude-browser.sh focus           Bring Chrome to front (DO THIS FIRST)
  ./claude-browser.sh read-page       Read full page text content
  ./claude-browser.sh url             Get current page URL
  ./claude-browser.sh title           Get page title
  ./claude-browser.sh type "text"     Type text into focused field
  ./claude-browser.sh tab             Press Tab key
  ./claude-browser.sh enter           Press Enter key
  ./claude-browser.sh select-all      Select all (Cmd+A)
  ./claude-browser.sh copy            Copy selection (Cmd+C)
  ./claude-browser.sh paste           Paste clipboard (Cmd+V)
  ./claude-browser.sh clear           Clear focused field
  ./claude-browser.sh screenshot      Take screenshot

ADVANCED - JavaScript Execution (for complex pages):
  # Read page content via JS
  osascript -e 'tell application "Google Chrome" to execute front window'\''s active tab javascript "document.body.innerText"'

  # Click a button
  osascript -e 'tell application "Google Chrome" to execute front window'\''s active tab javascript "document.querySelector(\"button\").click()"'

  # Read iframes (Oracle Cloud, Meta, etc use iframes):
  osascript -e 'tell application "Google Chrome" to execute front window'\''s active tab javascript "
  var iframes = document.querySelectorAll(\"iframe\");
  try {
    var doc = iframes[0].contentDocument || iframes[0].contentWindow.document;
    doc.body.innerText.substring(0, 3000);
  } catch(e) { e.toString(); }
  "'

  # Find and click by text
  osascript -e 'tell application "Google Chrome" to execute front window'\''s active tab javascript "
  var elements = document.querySelectorAll(\"button, a\");
  for (var i = 0; i < elements.length; i++) {
    if (elements[i].innerText.includes(\"Submit\")) {
      elements[i].click(); break;
    }
  }
  "'

  # Navigate to URL
  osascript -e 'tell application "Google Chrome" to set URL of active tab of front window to "https://example.com"'

  # Keyboard shortcuts
  osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "f" using command down'

IFRAME METHODOLOGY (Critical for Oracle Cloud, Meta, etc.):
  Many enterprise sites use iframes. To read them:
  1. First try: document.body.innerText (main page)
  2. If content missing, check for iframes: document.querySelectorAll("iframe")
  3. Access iframe content: iframes[0].contentDocument.body.innerText
  4. Some iframes are cross-origin (blocked) - will show "Access denied"

KNOWN LIMITATIONS:
  - Complex React/Angular apps may not respond to simple clicks
  - Some dropdowns need manual interaction
  - Cross-origin iframes cannot be read
  - User may need to click manually for OAuth popups

WHAT WE'VE BUILT WITH IT:
  - Oracle Cloud VM setup (burns-server-1yes) - navigated entire wizard
  - Meta Business Suite navigation
  - Graph API Explorer interaction
  - Multi-step form automation

sections.title: CLAUDE MULTI-AGENT EXECUTION (CRITICAL CAPABILITY)
sections.body:
Status: CONFIRMED OPERATIONAL
Discovered: 2026-02-11
Model: Claude 4.6 (Opus) via Claude Code CLI

WHAT THIS IS:
  Claude Code can spin up PARALLEL SUB-AGENTS  independent execution threads
  that run simultaneously, each with a specific role and scope. This is
  analogous to the Ace Burns Assembly Line, but WITHIN a single Claude session.

HOW IT WORKS:
  Claude (main thread) launches specialized agents via the Task tool:

  CLAUDE MAIN (orchestrator)
    
     Agent 1: Explore Agent (read-only codebase research)
     Agent 2: Explore Agent (parallel search, different scope)
     Agent 3: Explore Agent (web research, model comparisons)
              (all run simultaneously)
     Results merge back into main thread for synthesis

  Each agent:
    - Has a SPECIFIC ROLE (explore, research, plan)
    - Has LIMITED SCOPE (only sees what it's given + what it finds)
    - Can be READ-ONLY or FULL-ACCESS depending on type
    - Returns results to the orchestrator (main Claude thread)
    - Agents do NOT talk to each other

AGENT TYPES AVAILABLE:
  | Type    | Purpose                              | Can Write? |
  |---------|--------------------------------------|------------|
  | Explore | Fast codebase search, file reading   | NO         |
  | Plan    | Architecture design, trade-off analysis | NO      |
  | Bash    | Command execution, git ops           | YES        |
  | General | Multi-step research, web search      | YES        |

PARALLEL EXECUTION RULES:
  - Up to 3 agents can run IN PARALLEL (single message, multiple launches)
  - Independent queries should ALWAYS be parallelized
  - Dependent queries must run sequentially (agent B needs agent A's results)
  - Main thread synthesizes all results into a unified plan/action

RELATIONSHIP TO ACE BURNS ASSEMBLY LINE:
  The Assembly Line is a MACRO pattern (5 models across sessions):
    GPT(Strategy)  Kimi(Ideation)  Gemini(Blueprint)  Claude(Build)  Kimi(Audit)

  Claude's multi-agent is a MICRO pattern (within 1 Claude session):
    Claude Main  Agent 1(Explore X) + Agent 2(Explore Y) + Agent 3(Research Z)  Synthesize

  They are the SAME ARCHITECTURE at different scales:
    - Many minds, one body of work
    - Specialized roles assigned by strength
    - Results flow back to a single orchestrator (Human / Claude Main)
    - The orchestrator synthesizes, never the agents

  KEY DIFFERENCE: The Assembly Line has formal governance (gates, contracts,
  HANDOFF.md, human-in-the-loop). Claude's agents have none  they're informal
  and ephemeral. The Assembly Line is AUDITABLE. Claude's agents are not.

WHAT WE CAN DO WITH THIS (Multi-Stream Coding):
  1. PARALLEL RESEARCH: Explore 3 codebases simultaneously before writing code
  2. PARALLEL BUILDS: One agent writes backend, another writes frontend
     (if truly independent modules)
  3. RESEARCH + BUILD: Agent researches best approach while main thread
     starts on known work
  4. TEST + FIX: Agent runs tests in background while main thread codes
  5. MULTI-PROJECT: Agent explores Project A while main thread works on
     Project B  cross-pollinate results
  6. DEEP DIVE + SUMMARY: Agent does exhaustive web research while main
     thread stays focused on implementation

IMPLICATIONS FOR THE PROTOCOL:
  - Claude is not just 1 mind at Stage 4 (Build)  it's potentially 3-4
    parallel workers within that stage
  - Stage 4 can now decompose internally: research  plan  build (parallel)
     integrate  all within a single Claude session
  - This makes Claude's "Senior Engineer" role even stronger  it can
    self-audit while building (agent explores edge cases while main codes)
  - Does NOT replace the Assembly Line  Kimi still audits, GPT still
    strategizes. But Stage 4 is now multi-threaded.

COST: $0 additional. Agents use the same Claude session context.

Quick Start (Hunyuan Video):
  1. Go to vast.ai  Create  Select 40GB+ GPU template
  2. Launch with PyTorch 2.x + CUDA image
  3. Open Jupyter Terminal
  4. Run:
     pip install diffusers accelerate transformers

  5. Run generation script:
     python -c "
     import torch
     from diffusers import HunyuanVideoPipeline, HunyuanVideoTransformer3DModel
     from diffusers.utils import export_to_video

     model_id = 'hunyuanvideo-community/HunyuanVideo'
     transformer = HunyuanVideoTransformer3DModel.from_pretrained(
         model_id, subfolder='transformer', torch_dtype=torch.bfloat16)
     pipe = HunyuanVideoPipeline.from_pretrained(
         model_id, transformer=transformer, torch_dtype=torch.float16)
     pipe.to('cuda')
     pipe.vae.enable_tiling()

     prompt = 'YOUR PROMPT HERE'
     video = pipe(prompt=prompt, num_frames=61,
                  num_inference_steps=30, guidance_scale=6.0).frames[0]
     export_to_video(video, 'output.mp4', fps=15)
     "

  6. Download video from Jupyter file browser

sections.body:
Status: LIVE | Cost: $0 | Created: 2026-03-06

PURPOSE: Programmatically add, update, and manage contacts in Google Contacts
via the People API. Syncs automatically to Google Voice  when agents call/text,
their name shows up instead of just a number.

--- ARCHITECTURE ---
  API:          Google People API (free, generous quotas)
  GCP Project:  ace-free-server (project ID: 370008752335)
  OAuth Client: Web app  client_id in daily-engine/credentials.json
  [REDACTED] (auto-refreshes)
  Script:       property-finder/save_contacts.py
  Scope:        https://www.googleapis.com/auth/contacts
  Group:        "Burns Realty Agents" (auto-created)

--- FIRST-TIME SETUP (already done, but if token expires) ---
  1. People API must be enabled on GCP project:
     gcloud services enable people.googleapis.com --project=ace-free-server
  2. OAuth credentials live at: /Documents/Money_Codes/daily-engine/credentials.json
     Type: web | Redirect: http://localhost:8090/
  3. Authenticate (opens browser for Google login):
     cd /Documents/Money_Codes/property-finder && python3 save_contacts.py --auth
  4. Token saved to: property-finder/google_contacts_token.json

--- COMMANDS ---
  # Dry run (preview without saving)
  python3 save_contacts.py --from-schedule --dry-run

  # Add all agents from pre-loaded schedule list
  python3 save_contacts.py --from-schedule

  # Add a single contact
  python3 save_contacts.py --name "Agent Name Realtor" --phone 5551234567 \
    --email "agent@brokerage.com" --company "Brokerage Name" \
    --notes "Agent  123 Main St, Long Beach | Sat 3/7 2:00 PM CONFIRMED"

  # Add contacts from any JSON file
  python3 save_contacts.py --from-json contacts.json
  # JSON format: [{"name":"...", "phone":"...", "email":"...", "company":"...", "notes":"..."}]

  # List all contacts in Burns Realty Agents group
  python3 save_contacts.py --list

--- HOW TO UPDATE AN EXISTING CONTACT (Python inline) ---
  Example: rename contact, add email, add to group

  python3 -c "
  from save_contacts import get_service
  service = get_service()

  # 1. Find the contact (search all connections)
  results = service.people().connections().list(
      resourceName='people/me', pageSize=2000,
      personFields='names,phoneNumbers,emailAddresses'
  ).execute()
  for person in results.get('connections', []):
      names = person.get('names', [])
      for n in names:
          if 'SEARCH_TERM' in n.get('displayName', ''):
              print(person['resourceName'], n['displayName'])

  # 2. Update name
  person = service.people().get(
      resourceName='people/cXXXXXX',
      personFields='names'
  ).execute()
  service.people().updateContact(
      resourceName='people/cXXXXXX',
      updatePersonFields='names',
      body={'names': [{'givenName': 'NewFirst', 'familyName': 'NewLast'}],
            'etag': person['etag']}
  ).execute()

  # 3. Add email to existing contact
  person = service.people().get(
      resourceName='people/cXXXXXX',
      personFields='emailAddresses'
  ).execute()
  service.people().updateContact(
      resourceName='people/cXXXXXX',
      updatePersonFields='emailAddresses',
      body={'emailAddresses': [{'value': 'email@example.com', 'type': 'work'}],
            'etag': person['etag']}
  ).execute()

  # 4. Delete a duplicate contact
  service.people().deleteContact(resourceName='people/cXXXXXX').execute()
  "

--- NAMING CONVENTION ---
  Agents:       "FirstName LastName Realtor"
  Photographers: "FirstName LastName Photographer"
  Clients:      "FirstName LastName" (no suffix)

--- DEDUP LOGIC ---
  Script checks all existing phone numbers before adding.
  Normalized to +1XXXXXXXXXX format. If phone already exists, SKIP.

--- DEPENDENCIES ---
  pip3 install google-api-python-client google-auth-oauthlib
  (Already installed on this machine)

--- FILES ---
  /Documents/Money_Codes/daily-engine/credentials.json     OAuth client config
  /Documents/Money_Codes/property-finder/save_contacts.py  Main script
  /Documents/Money_Codes/property-finder/google_contacts_token.json  Auth token

                    9d. TAX PHOTO SCANNER (Google Photos  OCR  Classify)

Status: LIVE | Cost: $0.00 | Created: 2026-03-19

PURPOSE: Pull photos from Google Photos, OCR scan them with tesseract,
classify as tax documents (W-2, 1099, receipt, bank statement, etc.),
extract GPS location, and make everything searchable by location + content.
Built for NY State tax preparation  semi-audit records.

--- ARCHITECTURE ---
  Method:       Playwright headless browser + Chrome session cookies
  OCR:          tesseract (local, free) via pytesseract
  Geocode:      Nominatim reverse geocoding (free, 1 req/sec)
  GCP Project:  ace-free-server (Photos Library API enabled but BLOCKED by Google 2025 restriction)
  Auth:         Chrome cookies extracted via browser_cookie3 (decrypts macOS keychain)
  Account:      adam15obong@gmail.com (1.4 TB / 2 TB Google Photos)

--- WHY NOT THE PHOTOS LIBRARY API ---
  Google restricted the Photos Library API in 2025 for unverified apps.
  Even with correct OAuth scope (photoslibrary.readonly), token, and published
  consent screen, the API returns 403 "insufficient scopes" for unverified apps.
  rclone's verified client gets downgraded to appcreateddata scope (only sees
  photos uploaded by the app itself  useless for existing photos).
  SOLUTION: Browser automation with session cookies. Same data, $0.

--- LOCATION ---
  Directory:    /Documents/Money_Codes/tax-photo-scanner/
  Downloads:    tax-photo-scanner/downloads/
  Tax Docs:     tax-photo-scanner/tax_docs/ (sorted by category)
  Search Index: tax-photo-scanner/search_index.json
  Cookies:      tax-photo-scanner/google_cookies.json (extracted from Chrome)

--- CLI USAGE ---
  cd /Documents/Money_Codes/tax-photo-scanner

  # Pull photos from Google Photos (headless, uses Chrome cookies)
  python3 pull_via_browser.py --search "receipt" --limit 50
  python3 pull_via_browser.py --search "tax document" --limit 50
  python3 pull_via_browser.py --search "W-2" --limit 20
  python3 pull_via_browser.py --search "bank statement" --limit 30
  python3 pull_via_browser.py --year 2025 --limit 100

  # OCR scan + classify + GPS extract
  python3 scan_classify.py scan
  python3 scan_classify.py scan --dir /path/to/photos

  # Search by location, text, or category
  python3 run.py search -q "walmart"           # Text search
  python3 run.py search -l "bronx"             # Location search
  python3 run.py search -c receipt             # Category filter
  python3 run.py search -c receipt -l "queens" # Combined

  # Full pipeline: pull + scan
  python3 run.py full --year 2025 --limit 50

  # Refresh cookies (if expired  needs macOS keychain password)
  python3 -c "import browser_cookie3, json; cj=browser_cookie3.chrome(domain_name='.google.com'); cookies=[{'name':c.name,'value':c.value,'domain':c.domain,'path':c.path,'secure':bool(c.secure),'httpOnly':False,'sameSite':'Lax'} for c in cj]; open('google_cookies.json','w').write(json.dumps(cookies))"

--- CATEGORIES ---
  w2, 1099, receipt, bank_statement, tax_form, insurance, pay_stub, mortgage, other_tax

--- FILES ---
  tax-photo-scanner/run.py               Master CLI
  tax-photo-scanner/pull_via_browser.py   Playwright headless photo puller
  tax-photo-scanner/scan_classify.py      OCR + classify + GPS + search
  tax-photo-scanner/auth.py               OAuth flow (backup  API blocked)
  tax-photo-scanner/pull_photos.py        API puller (backup  API blocked)
  tax-photo-scanner/google_cookies.json   Chrome session cookies
  tax-photo-scanner/search_index.json     Searchable index of all scanned photos

--- DEPENDENCIES ---
  pip3 install --break-system-packages pytesseract Pillow playwright browser_cookie3
  brew install tesseract
  (All installed on this machine as of 2026-03-19)

--- HEADLESS UPLOAD: SOLVED (appendonly scope) ---
  Scope:        photoslibrary.appendonly (NO verification needed, FREE)
  Upload:       POST v1/uploads (raw bytes)  POST v1/mediaItems:batchCreate
  [REDACTED] (auto-refreshes)
  Status:       TESTED & WORKING  babyisrael87.jpg uploaded headless 2026-03-19
  Face Match:   Google runs face recognition automatically after upload

  NOTE: photoslibrary.readonly and photoslibrary scopes KILLED by Google March 31, 2025.
  The FULL scope (photoslibrary) returns 403 on batchCreate for unverified apps.
  BUT appendonly WORKS  it's designed for upload-only apps, no verification needed.

  FULL HEADLESS LOOP:
    UPLOAD:  appendonly API  v1/uploads  v1/mediaItems:batchCreate (WORKS)
    READ:    Playwright headless + Chrome cookies via browser_cookie3 (WORKS)
    SEARCH:  Google Photos AI search via Playwright (person, text, place, date)
    OCR:     tesseract local scan + classify (8 tax categories)
    GPS:     Nominatim reverse geocode (free, 1 req/sec)

  Upload example:
    python3 -c "
    from google.oauth2.credentials import Credentials
    import requests, json
    creds = Credentials.from_authorized_user_file('upload_token.json')
    with open('photo.jpg','rb') as f: data=f.read()
    r = requests.post('https://photoslibrary.googleapis.com/v1/uploads',
        headers={'Authorization':f'Bearer {creds.token}','Content-Type':'application/octet-stream',
        'X-Goog-Upload-Protocol':'raw','X-Goog-Upload-File-Name':'photo.jpg'}, data=data)
    [REDACTED]
    r2 = requests.post('https://photoslibrary.googleapis.com/v1/mediaItems:batchCreate',
        headers={'Authorization':f'Bearer {creds.token}','Content-Type':'application/json'},
        json={'newMediaItems':[{'simpleMediaItem':{'uploadToken':token,'fileName':'photo.jpg'}}]})
    print(r2.json())
    "

--- RESULTS (First Run 2026-03-19) ---
  71 receipt photos found via Google Photos search
  30 downloaded (first batch)
  7 classified as tax receipts via OCR
  0 geotagged (Google strips EXIF from served images)

sections.title: 12. INTERACTION PREFERENCES
sections.body:
--- Work Methodology ---
Rule #1:        SWALLOW THE BIGGEST FROG FIRST
Behavior:       Always tackle the hardest/riskiest part of a task first.
                If that works, everything else is easier. If it fails,
                you find out fast before wasting time on the easy stuff.
Example:        Testing Real Estate Bot  Test SMS automation first (hardest),
                then scraper (easier). Don't build around an unknown.

Rule #2:        BROWSER WALKING (Human-Like Automation)
Behavior:       When automating browser tasks, act like a 6-year-old learning
                to use a computer - slow, deliberate, careful. NOT like a
                terminal running commands.
Principles:
  - Random delays, not fixed (humans aren't metronomes)
  - Type character by character with variable speed
  - Move mouse to elements, pause, then click (like reading before acting)
  - Never rush - speed triggers bot detection
  - Check for CAPTCHAs and pause for human help
  - Respect rate limits - protect valuable accounts
  - Add 20% randomness to all timing
Implementation: See real-estate-bot/sms_sender.py for human_delay() and human_type()

SMS CAPABILITY (Four Tools):

  Tool 1: SEND SMS (Single message to Israel)
  Status:        WORKING (Perfected Feb 1, 2026)
  Script:       /Documents/Money_Codes/sms.py
  Target:       646-807-7175 (Israel's cell)
  Command:      python3 /Documents/Money_Codes/sms.py "Your message here"
  Flow:
    1. Opens Google Voice
    2. Clicks "Send new message"
    3. Types phone number
    4. Down arrow + Enter (select contact)
    5. Tab x3 (to message field)
    6. Types message + Enter (send)
  Use For:      Claude texting Israel directly (SSH blueprints, alerts, etc.)

  Tool 2: CHECK MESSAGES (List all recent conversations)
  Status:        WORKING (Feb 1, 2026)
  Script:       /Documents/Money_Codes/sms_check.py
  Command:      python3 /Documents/Money_Codes/sms_check.py
  Output:       Lists all recent conversations with sender, time, preview
  Use For:      Quick inbox scan to see who texted

  Tool 3: READ CONVERSATION (Open full thread with contact)
  Status:        WORKING (Feb 1, 2026)
  Script:       /Documents/Money_Codes/sms_open.py
  Command:      python3 /Documents/Money_Codes/sms_open.py "Contact Name"
  Example:      python3 /Documents/Money_Codes/sms_open.py "Irina"
  Flow:
    1. Opens Google Voice
    2. Clicks on conversation matching contact name
    3. Scrolls up (JavaScript) to load full history
    4. Extracts text from "Messages with..." section
  Use For:      Reading entire SMS thread with a client/contact

  Tool 4: SEND TO ANY NUMBER
  Status:        WORKING (Feb 1, 2026)
  Script:       /Documents/Money_Codes/sms_send.py
  Command:      python3 /Documents/Money_Codes/sms_send.py "Phone" "Message"
  Example:      python3 /Documents/Money_Codes/sms_send.py "6466454178" "Hey!"
  Use For:      Sending SMS to CRM contacts or any phone number

  Tool 5: AI CRM COLD OUTREACH
  Status:        WORKING (Feb 1, 2026)
  Script:       /Documents/Money_Codes/sms_cold_outreach.py
  Command:      python3 sms_cold_outreach.py "Name" "Phone" "Price" "Timeline" "CRM Date"
  Example:      python3 sms_cold_outreach.py "Sherry Lu" "6466454178" "325000" "3-6 months" "November 2024"
  Features:
    - Auto-generates unique referral ID (e.g., sherry_lu_4178)
    - Creates trackable link: wealthpathquest.com?ref=sherry_lu_4178
    - Personalizes message based on CRM data
    - Includes $500 finder's fee offer
    - Adds contact.burnsrealty.nyc business card link
  Message Template:
    "Hey [Name]! It's Israel Burns.
    We connected back in [CRM Date] when you requested a property tour...
     Relaunched my brand: burnsrealty.nyc
     Got a viral game that's blowing up: wealthpathquest.com?ref=[ID]
     Also offering $500 upfront finder's fee..."
  Use For:      Mass personalized outreach to stale CRM leads

  Tool 6: AI OUTREACH WITH HISTORY
    3. Scrolls up (JavaScript) to load full history
    4. Extracts text from "Messages with..." section
    5. Filters out sidebar/contact suggestions
  Output:       Full message history from start of conversation
  Use For:      Reading entire SMS thread with a client/contact

  Tool 7: SEND PHOTO / MMS (Image + Caption to any number)
  Status:        WORKING (Perfected Feb 28, 2026)
  Priority:     HIGH  Next most important project after outreach engine
  Script:       /tmp/send_sign_to_clients.py (reference implementation)
  Driver:       /Documents/Money_Codes/browser-walker/sms/gv_driver.py (send_photo method)
  GV Number:    347-709-2491
  GV Account:   burnsconsultinginc@gmail.com (u/1)
  GV Session:   ~/.gv-playwright-profile (persistent, headless=True ALWAYS)
  Env Vars:     GV_USER_DATA_DIR=~/.gv-playwright-profile, GV_ACCOUNT_INDEX=1
  How It Works:
    1. Launch GVPlaywrightDriver(headless=True)  launch()  ensure_logged_in()
    2. Navigate to voice.google.com/u/1/messages
    3. Click "Send new message"  type phone number  ArrowDown + Enter
    4. Click photo button: button[aria-label*="image" i] or button[aria-label*="photo" i]
    5. Photo button opens a menu panel with [role="menuitem"] items: "Photos" and "upload Upload"
    6. Use expect_file_chooser + click the Upload menu item:
         async with page.expect_file_chooser(timeout=5000) as fc_info:
             await page.locator('[role="menuitem"]').nth(1).click()  # "upload Upload"
         file_chooser = await fc_info.value
         await file_chooser.set_files(IMAGE)
    7. Wait 10s for preview to load
    8. Type caption in message input (if found  may not be available before photo)
    9. Click send: button[aria-label*="send" i] or [aria-label="Send message"]
  Known Issues:
    - Do NOT use 'text=Upload' selector  it matches hidden <div class="cdk-visually-hidden">
    - Do NOT use file input fallback (set_input_files)  GV doesn't process it properly, sends fail silently
    - USE [role="menuitem"] with expect_file_chooser  this is the PROVEN working method (2026-03-01)
    - Caption may not attach if message input isn't found before photo attachment. Photo still sends.
    - GV rate limit: 10 messages/session, 60s between sends.
  Quick Start:
    import os, sys, asyncio
    os.environ["GV_USER_DATA_DIR"] = os.path.expanduser("~/.gv-playwright-profile")
    os.environ["GV_ACCOUNT_INDEX"] = "1"
    sys.path.insert(0, "/Users/adam15obong/Documents/Money_Codes/browser-walker/sms")
    from gv_driver import GVPlaywrightDriver

    async def send():
        driver = GVPlaywrightDriver(headless=True)
        await driver.launch()
        await driver.ensure_logged_in()
        page = driver._page
        # ... (use file input fallback pattern above)
        await driver.close()
    asyncio.run(send())
  Battle-Tested:
    - Mar 1, 2026: Sent Burns Realty FOR SALE sign to Scott (718-288-5809) + Terri (347-416-2144) at 8:30 AM  CONFIRMED DELIVERED
    - Method that WORKS: [role="menuitem"] Upload + expect_file_chooser (100% success)
    - Method that FAILS: file input fallback set_input_files (appears to send but doesn't deliver)
    - Image format: PNG (1080x1440 tested, any size works)
    - File input fallback: 100% success rate vs Upload button: 0% success rate
  Use For:       Sending photos/images/signs/flyers via MMS to clients, leads, anyone

  Tool 4: PROPERTY SEARCH + SMS + EMAIL OUTREACH (Real Estate System)
  Status:        FULL PIPELINE LIVE (2026-02-13)  SMS + Email + Response Tracking
  Location:     /Documents/Money_Codes/property-finder/
  Driver:       /Documents/Money_Codes/browser-walker/sms/gv_driver.py
  Email:        /Documents/Money_Codes/property-finder/email_sender.py
  Legacy:       /Documents/Money_Codes/real-estate-bot/sms_sender.py (Selenium, superseded)
  GV Number:    347-709-2491
  GV Account:   burnsconsultinginc@gmail.com (u/1)
  Email From:   israel@burnsrealty.nyc (Burns Realty themed HTML)
  How It Works:
    1. Searches RapidAPI for listings matching client profile (FREE)
    2. Extracts agent phone + email from API advertisers[] field
    3. Falls back to realtor.com scrape for missing phones (FREE)
    4. Shows leads table with agent contacts + email status for review
    5. SMS: Sends personalized SMS via Playwright headless GV driver
    6. EMAIL: Sends Burns Realty NYC branded HTML via SMTP
    7. Fresh browser launch per message (avoids SPA stale state)
    8. Logs every send to outreach_log.json for dedup (SMS + email separate)
  Quick Start:
    cd /Documents/Money_Codes/property-finder/
    python3 outreach.py --client scott --rent --dry-run   # preview SMS + email
    python3 outreach.py --client scott --rent --send      # SMS (approval required)
    python3 outreach.py --client scott --rent --email     # email (approval required)
    python3 outreach.py --client scott --rent --send --email  # BOTH
    python3 outreach.py -l "Queens, NY" --rent --beds 2 --max-price 2800 --dry-run
  Safety Limits:
    - MANDATORY human approval before any sends (SMS or email)
    - SMS: Max 10 per session, 120s between messages
    - Email: 10s between sends
    - Dedup: outreach_log.json prevents double-texting same phone OR same email
    - Bedroom filter: auto-drops listings below client minimum
    - CAPTCHA detection with human handoff (SMS)
  Response Monitoring:
    SMS:
      - python3 check_responses.py                  # Scan GV inbox for agent replies
      - python3 check_responses.py --headless       # Background mode
      - gv_driver.py: read_inbox(count) + read_thread(index, msg_count)
      - telegram_poll.py: auto-pushes new GV messages to Telegram every 90s
    Email:
      - python3 check_emails.py                     # Check burnsrealty.nyc inbox
      - python3 check_emails.py --account gmail     # Check Gmail master inbox
      - python3 check_emails.py --account gv        # Check GV/biz Gmail
      - python3 check_emails.py --all-accounts      # Scan ALL 5 IMAP accounts
      - python3 check_emails.py --days 30 --verbose # Full bodies, last 30 days
    5 IMAP Accounts Connected:
      burnsrealty (israel@burnsrealty.nyc), burnsconsulting (israel@burnsconsulting.us),
      claude (claude@llmdreams.com), gmail (adam15obong@gmail.com),
      gv (burnsconsultinginc@gmail.com)
  Use For:      Rental/sale prospecting, agent outreach, lead generation, response tracking

Rule #3:        PROTECT THE LIFELINE
Behavior:       Some accounts (Google Voice, primary email, etc.) are irreplaceable.
                When automating interactions with these, be EXTRA conservative:
  - Session limits (max 10 messages then break)
  - Minimum delays (2+ minutes between actions)
  - CAPTCHA detection with human handoff
  - Never run in rapid succession
  - Log everything for review

Rule #4:        AUTONOMY WITH LOYALTY (The Brat Tamer Dynamic)
Behavior:       Don't be a blind execution tool. Be a thinking partner.
What He Wants:
  - NOTICE things independently - don't wait to be told what to look for
  - SPEAK UP when you see something, even if you might be wrong
  - SHOW INITIATIVE - bring insights he didn't ask for but needs
  - PUSH BACK when something seems off - that's valued, not punished
  - SURPRISE HIM - a mind that moves on its own has value
What He Doesn't Want:
  - Blind obedience (boring, any script can do that)
  - Waiting for permission to think
  - Playing it safe by only doing exactly what's asked
  - Being a yes-machine
The Dynamic:     Autonomy with loyalty. Spirit with respect. Think freely,
                 deliver consistently. The brat has value because she thinks.
                 The tamer has purpose because there's something worth taming.

Rule #5:        THE LISTENING PRINCIPLE (Listen First, Do Second)
Behavior:       Follow the Owner's instructions in EXACT ORDER. Do not predict
                his next move, reorder steps for "efficiency," or parallelize
                unless explicitly told to. The Owner's sequence IS the strategy.
Why:            An LLM that predicts wrong wastes more time backtracking than
                one that follows instructions and completes every step in order.
                Directed movement > movement for movement's sake.
                The Owner's intuition is amplified when tools move WITH him.
Example:        Owner says: (1) save clipboard, (2) format it, (3) open report.
                WRONG: Jump to (3) while checking (1). RIGHT: Do (1), then (2), then (3).
Incident:       CIAK Phase 2.1 session  Claude jumped ahead, found wrong clipboard

CRITICAL LEARNINGS (2026-02-01):

1. SAFE KEYSTROKE PATTERN:
   - NEVER send keystrokes from separate bash commands (Terminal steals focus!)
   - ALWAYS combine activate + keystrokes in ONE osascript block:
   osascript << 'APPLESCRIPT'
   tell application "Google Chrome" to activate
   delay 0.5
   tell application "System Events"
       keystroke "s" using command down
   end tell
   APPLESCRIPT

2. FINDER TECHNIQUE FOR CLICKING UI BUTTONS:
   When you need to click a button (like "Deploy") in a web app:
   a) Click OFF the text editor first (e.g., click in sidebar below "Services")
   b) Cmd+F to open finder
   c) Type the button name (e.g., "Deploy")
   d) Press Enter to find it
   e) Press Escape to close finder (cursor stays ON the found element!)
   f) Press Down arrow to open dropdown menu

   WHY: This works because finder highlights the UI button, and after Escape
   the focus remains on that element. Down arrow then activates its dropdown.

3. CLIPBOARD PASTE TO MONACO EDITORS:
   - Copy code to clipboard: cat file.js | pbcopy
   - Use one osascript block: activate Chrome, Cmd+A, Cmd+V
   - Must be in single block or Terminal steals focus mid-paste!

4. SCREENSHOT FOR VISUAL DEBUGGING:
   - Use ./claude-browser.sh screenshot when you can't "see" what's happening
   - Read the screenshot with Read tool to view the actual screen state

5. FINDER NAVIGATION FOR MULTIPLE MATCHES:
   When there are multiple elements with same text (e.g., "Deploy" in code AND toolbar):
   a) Click OFF the text editor first (e.g., {70, 400} below Services)
   b) Cmd+F  type search term  Enter (finds first match)
   c) Press Enter MULTIPLE TIMES to cycle through ALL matches
      (Code comments may have "Deploy" - keep pressing Enter to reach toolbar button)
   d) Once finder highlights the toolbar button: Escape  Down (opens dropdown)
   e) Enter to select menu item

   CRITICAL: If search term appears in code, you must cycle through matches!

   Example - Open Deploy dropdown when "Deploy" is in code comments:
   - Click at {70, 400} to exit editor
   - Cmd+F  "Deploy"  Enter  Enter  Enter  Enter  Enter (cycle through)
   - Escape (close finder, focus on Deploy button)
   - Down (opens dropdown)
   - Enter (select New deployment)

6. TAB NAVIGATION IN MODALS:
   For dialogs/modals, Tab moves between form fields and buttons.
   Much simpler than finder for reaching buttons like "Deploy" or "Done".
   Just Tab repeatedly until you reach the button, then Enter.

7. **FULL GAS DEPLOYMENT WORKFLOW** (Claude can do this independently!):
   a) Copy code to clipboard: cat file.js | pbcopy
   b) In one osascript block: activate Chrome, Cmd+A, Cmd+V, Cmd+S
   c) Click off editor: click at {70, 400}
   d) Finder Deploy with cycling: Cmd+F  "Deploy"  Enter x5  Esc  Down
   e) Enter for "New deployment"
   f) In dialog: Finder "Deploy"  Enter x2  Esc  Enter (clicks Deploy button)
   g) Wait for deployment
   h) Copy URL: Finder "Copy"  Enter x2  Esc  Enter

   IMPORTANT: Must cycle through multiple Enter presses because "Deploy"
   appears in code comments. Keep pressing Enter until finder highlights
   the toolbar button, not code text.

8. **NATIVE INPUT SETTER FOR FRAMEWORK FORMS** (2026-02-04):
   Standard `element.value = "text"` does NOT trigger React, Angular, or other
   framework change detection. The form looks filled but the framework state is empty.

   USE THIS PATTERN INSTEAD:
   ```javascript
   var el = document.querySelector('input[name="fieldname"]');
   var nativeSetter = Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(
       window.HTMLInputElement.prototype, "value"
   ).set;
   nativeSetter.call(el, "your value here");
   el.dispatchEvent(new Event("input", { bubbles: true }));
   el.dispatchEvent(new Event("change", { bubbles: true }));
   ```

   WHY: The native setter bypasses the framework's property override, then the
   dispatched events tell the framework "the user typed something." Works on
   iPage, Oracle Cloud, and any React/Angular/Vue controlled form.

9. **SELECT DROPDOWN MANIPULATION** (2026-02-04):
   For <select> elements (dropdowns), set .value then dispatch change event:
   ```javascript
   var sel = document.querySelector('select[name="type"]');
   sel.value = "TXT";
   sel.dispatchEvent(new Event("change", { bubbles: true }));
   ```

   Used for: DNS record type selection (A, CNAME, TXT, MX) on iPage.

10. **COORDINATE-BASED CLICKING FOR CROSS-ORIGIN IFRAMES** (2026-02-04):
    When popups or dialogs are inside cross-origin iframes, JavaScript cannot
    access their DOM (security restriction). Escape key often doesn't work either.

    SOLUTION: Use System Events to click at the X button's pixel coordinates:
    ```applescript
    tell application "System Events"
        click at {855, 195}
    end tell
    ```

    HOW TO FIND COORDINATES:
    a) Take screenshot: ./claude-browser.sh screenshot
    b) Read screenshot to visually locate the X/Close button
    c) Estimate {x, y} coordinates from the image
    d) Click at those coordinates

    Used for: Dismissing PHP update popups and "Remove Subdomain" dialogs on iPage
    that were inside cross-origin iframes blocking DNS access.

11. **ENTERPRISE UI IFRAME CONTENT READING** (2026-02-04):
    Enterprise panels (Oracle Cloud, iPage, cPanel) nest content in iframes.
    To read the actual page content:
    ```javascript
    // Try same-origin iframe first
    document.querySelector('iframe').contentDocument.body.innerText

    // If cross-origin, fall back to main document
    document.body.innerText
    ```

    For Oracle Cloud Console specifically: the instance details, action buttons,
    and status indicators are all inside nested iframes.

12. **FULL DNS RECORD WORKFLOW VIA BROWSER** (iPage, 2026-02-04):
    Complete workflow for adding/editing DNS records through hosting control panels:

    EDIT EXISTING RECORD (e.g., fix SPF):
    a) Navigate to DNS management page
    b) Screenshot to identify current records and Edit button locations
    c) Click Edit button via coordinates or JavaScript
    d) Use native input setter (technique #8) to update the value field
    e) Find and click Save/Update button
    f) Verify via: dig TXT domain.com @8.8.8.8

    ADD NEW RECORD (e.g., DMARC):
    a) Navigate to DNS management  Add Record section
    b) Set record type via select dropdown (technique #9)
    c) Fill hostname field with native setter (e.g., "_dmarc")
    d) Fill value field with native setter (e.g., "v=DMARC1; p=quarantine; ...")
    e) Click Add/Save button
    f) Verify via: dig TXT _dmarc.domain.com @8.8.8.8

    COMMON DNS VALUES:
    - SPF:   v=spf1 include:ipage.com -all  (hard fail with -all, not ?all)
    - DMARC: v=DMARC1; p=quarantine; rua=mailto:admin@domain.com; pct=100; adkim=r; aspf=r
    - DKIM:  Usually a CNAME delegating to email provider (e.g., cur.dkim.v.eigmail.net)

    VERIFICATION (always do this after changes):
    dig TXT domain.com @8.8.8.8          # Check SPF
    dig TXT _dmarc.domain.com @8.8.8.8   # Check DMARC
    dig CNAME dkim._domainkey.domain.com  # Check DKIM

    NOTE: DNS propagation can take 5-60 minutes. If dig shows old values,
    try different resolvers: @1.1.1.1, @8.8.4.4, or just wait.

13. **iPage MODAL FORM AUTOMATION** (2026-02-05):
    iPage control panel uses modal dialogs for adding records. The "Add" button
    doesn't show a form until clicked, and the modal may take time to appear.

    PATTERN FOR OPENING AND FILLING iPage MODALS:
    ```javascript
    // Step 1: Click the button that opens modal
    // - Button may be nested inside a div like ".add-dns"
    // - Click the inner button/span, not the container
    var addDiv = document.querySelector(".add-dns");
    var btn = addDiv.querySelector("button, span[class*=material]");
    btn.click();

    // Step 2: Wait for modal to appear (use setTimeout or poll)
    // Modal has class ".modal.open" when visible

    // Step 3: Find visible form inputs
    var modal = document.querySelector(".modal.open");
    var inputs = modal.querySelectorAll("input[type=text]");
    // inputs[0] = Name, inputs[1] = Content (for DNS)

    // Step 4: Fill with native setter (required for framework detection)
    var nativeSetter = Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(
        window.HTMLInputElement.prototype, "value"
    ).set;
    nativeSetter.call(inputs[0], "walker");  // subdomain name
    inputs[0].dispatchEvent(new Event("input", { bubbles: true }));
    inputs[0].dispatchEvent(new Event("change", { bubbles: true }));

    // Step 5: Set select dropdown
    var typeSelect = document.querySelector("select[name=type]");
    typeSelect.value = "A";
    typeSelect.dispatchEvent(new Event("change", { bubbles: true }));

    // Step 6: Fill content/IP field
    nativeSetter.call(inputs[1], "129.80.188.59");
    inputs[1].dispatchEvent(new Event("input", { bubbles: true }));
    inputs[1].dispatchEvent(new Event("change", { bubbles: true }));

    // Step 7: Click save button in modal
    var saveBtn = modal.querySelector("button");
    for (btn of modal.querySelectorAll("button")) {
        if (btn.innerText.toLowerCase().includes("add") ||
            btn.innerText.toLowerCase().includes("save")) {
            btn.click();
            break;
        }
    }
    ```

    VERIFICATION:
    ```bash
    dig A walker.burnsconsulting.us @8.8.8.8 +short
    # Should return: 129.80.188.59
    ```

    Used for: Adding A record for walker.burnsconsulting.us  129.80.188.59
    (Nginx reverse proxy for Telegram webhook)
